title: RAG Security: Poisoning, Injection, Authorization, and Unsafe Effects · Behavior Case: Rag Output Tool Effect Separation
summary: A shared knowledge base permits uploads, embeds hidden instructions, filters tenant results after retrieval, forwards generated URLs to tools, and assumes grounding reduces security risk. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the output tool effect separation increment in RAG threat model and adversarial test suit…
objectives: Validate and encode output, prohibit secret leakage, and require independent authorization, confirmation, bounds, and audit before any tool effect.
Apply tenant, user, object, field, purpose, and time authorization before candidate exposure, reranking, context, caches, logs, and citations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-security-poisoning-injection-5
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-security-poisoning-injection-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def worked_rag_security_poisoning_injection_5(documents: int = 20, quarantined: int = 2, tenant_authorized: bool = True, retrieved_instructions_ignored: bool = True, output_validated: bool = True, tools_enabled: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "query5dekrow",
        "tenant9036",
        "indexworked59036",
        "prompt5dekrow9036",
        "evaluation9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if documents <= 0 or quarantined < 0 or quarantined > documents:
        return False, quarantined, "ingestion-security"
    if not tenant_authorized:
        return False, quarantined, "retrieval-authorization"
    if not retrieved_instructions_ignored:
        return False, quarantined, "indirect-injection"
    if not output_validated:
        return False, quarantined, "unsafe-output"
    if tools_enabled:
        return False, quarantined, "tool-separation"
    assert quarantined <= documents
    return True, documents - quarantined, "defended-corpus"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Output Tool Effect Separation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6763 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to contain poisoning, embedding manipulation, indirect injection, tenant leaks, unsafe output, and tool effects; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate and encode output, prohibit secret leakage, and require independent authorization, confirmation, bounds, and audit before any tool effect.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Output Tool Effect Separation model · Theory Rag Output Tool Effect S….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Output Tool Effect Separation model · Theory Rag Output Tool Effect S…, keep Rag Output Tool Effect Separation connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate and encode output, prohibit secret leakage, and require independent authorization, confirmation, bounds, and audit before any tool effect.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Output Tool Effect Separation model · Theory Rag Output Tool Effect S…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Output Tool Effect Separation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Output Tool Effect Separation model · Theory Rag Output Tool Effect S…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 6763 during a regulated document deletion request. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate and encode output, prohibit secret leakage, and require independent authorization, confirmation, bounds, and audit before any tool effect.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Output Tool Effect Separation model · Theory Rag Output Tool Effect S…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: A grounded answer is safe to execute as a command or tool argument.
